perturbation.original_text: A. "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time of three (3) years commencing on November 1st, 2017 and concluding November 2nd, 2020, unless terminated sooner or extended as provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time of three (3) years commencing on November 1st, 2017 and concluding November 2nd, 2020, and will automatically renew for subsequent three-year terms, unless terminated sooner as provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an automatic renewal clause to the definition of 'Contract Period.' This contradicts Section Fifteen which outlines the extension process requiring discussions, not automatic renewal. The contradiction creates uncertainty about the contract's term length and renewal conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION FIFTEEN. CONTRACT EXTENSION Due to long product development lead times, Company and NFLA-NC agree to begin discussions for the renewal of this Agreement by no later than June 1st, 2020. All terms of this Agreement will automatically commence on November 1st, 2017, and expire on November 2nd, 2020.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause to the 'Contract Period' definition, directly conflicting with Section Fifteen, which stipulates that contract extension requires discussions and does not mention automatic renewal. The original text defined a clear end date, while the changed text introduces an open-ended term subject to termination only. This creates a significant contradiction, as Section Fifteen outlines a renewal process based on discussion, while the changed text implies automatic renewal unless terminated. This direct clash between requiring discussion versus automatic renewal creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: NFLA agrees not to grant the right to use the NFLAs Identification to anyone other than Company in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products.
perturbation.changed_text: NFLA agrees not to grant the exclusive right to use the NFLAs Identification to anyone other than Company in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'exclusive' to the agreement, creates in-text contradiction with Section 12 (Reservation of Rights). This addition, implies that NFLA can still authorize other entities to use the NFLA Identification but not 'exclusively', creating uncertainty about the scope of the Company's rights and the extent to which the NFLA can partner with other companies for similar endorsements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION TWELVE. RESERVATION OF RIGHTS All rights not specifically granted in this Agreement to Company shall remain the property of the NFLA to be used in any manner the NFLA deems appropriate. Company understands that the NFLA has reserved the right to authorize others to use the name of the NFLA within the Contract Territory and during the Contract Period in connection with all tangible and intangible items and services other than Products themselves. NFLA is not aware of any such rights that would conflict with the nature or image of Company Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented NFLA from granting the right to use their Identification to anyone other than the Company. The changed text only prevents the grant of *exclusive* rights. This directly contradicts Section 12 (Reservation of Rights), which explicitly states that NFLA reserves the right to authorize others to use the name of the NFLA in connection with items and services other than Products. The change introduces the possibility of NFLA granting non-exclusive rights to others, directly conflicting with the reservation of rights clause. This makes the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable as to the scope of rights granted to Company.

perturbation.original_text: All payments shall be made by wire transfer drawn to the account of NFLA-NC no later than ten (10) business days after the end of each quarter as follows: $0.05 per Unit as described herein of Company's Products sold in the Contract Territory payable to NFLA-NC.
perturbation.changed_text: All payments shall be made via mutually agreed method (wire transfer, check or other means) drawn to the account of NFLA-NC no later than thirty (30) business days after the end of each quarter as follows: $0.05 per Unit as described herein of Company's Products sold in the Contract Territory payable to NFLA-NC.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that all payments be made via wire transfer within ten days after each quarter. This change introduces conflicting ideas; a 'mutually agreed upon method,' implies it could be any agreed upon method while extending the payment deadline to thirty business days which introduces conflict and ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. If either party at any time during the Contract Period shall: (i) fail to make any payment of any sum of money specified in this Agreement to be made; or (ii) fail to observe or perform any of the covenants, agreements or obligations under this Agreement (other than the payment of money), the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: As to a default under clause (i) above, if such payment is not made within 10 business days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment; or, as to a default under clause (ii) above, if such other default is not cured within 30 days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment deadline to thirty (30) business days and introduces 'mutually agreed method', while the contradicted text states that failure to make payment within ten (10) business days after notice is grounds for termination. This creates a direct conflict regarding the allowed payment timeframe, which aligns with definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. The changed text creates uncertainty about payment obligations, which could lead to disputes and potential termination based on contradictory deadlines.
